app.name: jzero
app.one_liner: A Go-based code generation framework built on go-zero that automates API, gateway, and RPC project scaffolding using descriptive files (api/proto/sql) and AI-assisted business logic.

screens.name: Project Initialization & Code Generation
screens.purpose: Allows developers to create new microservice projects or regenerate code from updated specs using CLI or Docker.
primary_actions: Run service locally with Swagger UI access

rust_commands.name: check_dependencies
rust_commands.purpose: Validates and installs required tooling dependencies for jzero operation.

rust_commands.name: generate_code
rust_commands.purpose: Generates server and client code from descriptive files (proto/api/sql) using built-in or custom templates.

rust_commands.name: new_project
rust_commands.purpose: Creates a new jzero-based microservice project scaffold from templates.

mvp_plan: week 1: Create initial project scaffold using 'jzero new'
week 1: Define basic API/proto descriptors for core service
week 1: Generate server/client code and validate structure
week 1: Set up jzero CLI environment via 'go install' or Docker
week 1: Start local service and verify Swagger UI accessibility
acceptance_tests: Code generation respects git-diff awareness and file filtering as described in README.
Generated project compiles and starts with 'go run main.go server'.
Running 'jzero new test_project' creates a valid directory structure with go.mod and main.go.
Swagger UI is accessible at http://localhost:8001/swagger after startup.
User can install jzero via 'go install' and run 'jzero check' without errors.
